channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Two independent audits confirm NordVPN as top tool for blocking malicious sites
item.description: NordVPN Threat Protection Pro proved its capabilities in malware and phishing protection, yet again. Here's all you need to know.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Australia won't force social media users to share their personal details when child ban takes effect
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia's communications minister said Wednesday the government won't force social media users to hand over their personal information to tech companies, as children younger than 16 are set to be banned from the platforms.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Must-do privacy settings on your iPhone in iOS 18.1
item.description: Apple's latest iOS update introduces advanced artificial intelligence capabilities, which may be capturing and analyzing sensitive information.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meta creates ‘Business AI’ group led by ex-Salesforce AI CEO Clara Shih
item.description: Meta has hired the former CEO of Salesforce AI, Clara Shih, to lead a new “Business AI” group. Salesforce has still not publicly acknowledged Shih’s departure, but it will be hard to ignore now that she’s announced her new role on her LinkedIn profile.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Why the crypto community loves Howard Lutnick, Trump’s pick for Commerce secretary
item.description: Donald Trump’s selection on Tuesday of Howard Lutnick as Commerce secretary brought cheers from the cryptocurrency sector. Little surprise there. Lutnick has long advocated for cryptocurrency to be treated on equal footing with other financial assets.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Overhaul of security clearance and personnel vetting process enters new phase
item.description: The Performance Accountability Council latest's report on “Trusted Workforce 2.0 said agencies have updated all major policies related to personnel vetting. The post Overhaul of security clearance and personnel vetting process enters new phase first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: CMA gives Google’s $2B Anthropic investment the green light
item.description: It took the UK’s Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) less than a month to decide that there is no need to proceed further with a merger investigation after Google’s purchase of a $2 billion stake in Anthropic. In a statement released on Oct.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What to know about Howard Lutnick, Trump's pick for commerce secretary
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has chosen Howard Lutnick, head of brokerage and investment bank Cantor Fitzgerald and cryptocurrency enthusiast, as his nominee for commerce secretary.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Sweeping VA reform bill with pay flexibility for health care workers passes House
item.description: Lawmakers axed language in the House-passed Dole Act that set higher standards for the VA to resume the rollout of its troubled new Electronic Health Record. The post Sweeping VA reform bill with pay flexibility for health care workers passes House first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Foxconn takes another big step toward AI iPhone manufacturing
item.description: Apple’s main manufacturing partner, Foxconn, has announced it is working with Nvidia to build digital twins that it says will reshape the future of manufacturing and supply chain management.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Forget driverless cars. One company wants autonomous helicopters to spray crops and fight fires
item.description: HENNIKER, N.H. (AP) — When Hector Xu was learning to fly a helicopter in college, he recalled having a few “nasty experiences” while trying to navigate at night.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: xAI Shakes Up AI Server Market with Switch from Supermicro to Dell
item.description: Elon Musk's xAI redirects orders from Supermicro to Dell, boosting Dell and its suppliers while setting back Supermicro.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: German police break up an environmental activists' camp near Tesla plant outside Berlin
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German police broke up a protest camp housing environmental activists in a forest near the Tesla electric car factory in Grünheide outside Berlin on Tuesday, citing violations of public safety and order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Akka distributed computing platform adds Java SDK
item.description: Akka, formerly known as Lightbend, has released Akka 3, an update to the JVM-based distributed computing platform that adds a Java SDK as well as serverless and “bring your own cloud” deployment options. Akka 3 and the company’s name change were announced November 15.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nvidia Blackwell chips face serious heating issues
item.description: Nvidia’s next-generation Blackwell data center processors have significant problems with overheating when installed in high-capacity server racks, forcing redesigns of the racks themselves, according to a report by The Information.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Company affiliated with Alex Jones seeks to disqualify The Onion's auction bid on Infowars
item.description: A company affiliated with conspiracy theorist Alex Jones asked a federal judge on Monday to disqualify a bid by the satirical news outlet The Onion to buy Jones' Infowars at a bankruptcy auction, alleging fraud and collusion.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Judge tosses Huckabee lawsuit against Meta over ads suggesting he endorsed marijuana gummies
item.description: WILMINGTON, Del. (AP) — A federal judge in Delaware on Monday dismissed a lawsuit filed by former Arkansas Gov. Mike Huckabee against social media giant Meta over advertisements using his name and image to sell CBD products.
item.dc:creator: RANDALL CHASE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Netflix says 60 million watched Tyson, Paul fight
item.description: About 60 million households tuned in to watch a prime-time prize fight between Jake Paul and Mike Tyson on Friday, Netflix said over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: How to make your VPN faster
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how a VPN — virtual private network — works and how running it can slow down the operation of your device.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Millions in high speed internet grants awarded in rural New York
item.description: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer and Gov. Kathy Hochul announced the funds for open-access fiber, wireless hubs.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: One Tech Tip: Replacing passwords with passkeys for an easier login experience
item.description: LONDON (AP) — If you're tired of memorizing passwords, then give passkeys a try. You might have noticed that many online services are now offering the option of using passkeys, a digital authentication method touted as an easier and more secure way to log in.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk says he and Trump have 'mandate to delete' regulations. Ethics laws could limit Musk role
item.description: In picking billionaire Elon Musk to be “our cost cutter” for the U.S. government, President-elect Donald Trump won't be the first American president to empower a business tycoon to look for ways to dramatically cut federal regulations. President Ronald Reagan tapped J.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, Fox 5
